hub.title: Set up dSVDC Hub
hub.description: Enter the port the virtualDC host will listen on. Your digitalSTROM server (dSS) must be able to reach this port on the Home Assistant host.

wait_for_dss.title: Waiting for dSS Connection
wait_for_dss.description: The virtualDC host is now announced on the network. Waiting for a digitalSTROM server to connect (timeout: 2 minutes)…

state_files.title: Existing State Files Found

data.action: What would you like to do?

abort.no_dss_found: No digitalSTROM server connected within 2 minutes. The virtualDC host has been stopped and the port released. Check that the dSS is on the same network and try again.
abort.cannot_connect: Could not start the virtualDC host on the selected port. The port may have become unavailable.

config_entry_name.title: Physical Device Name
config_entry_name.description: Enter a name for the physical device. This name is used only in Home Assistant to identify your device and is not reported to digitalSTROM.

data_description.entry_name: Label for this physical device as it will appear in Home Assistant. Example: 'Smart Livingroom Light'.

creation_mode.title: How to Create the Virtual Device
creation_mode.description: A virtual digitalSTROM device (vdSD) bridges your Home Assistant device to the digitalSTROM ecosystem. Choose how you want to define it.

data_description.mode: 'From HA entities' maps one HA entity at a time — best for precise control. 'From HA device' imports all supported entities of an existing HA device at once. 'From scratch' lets you define every component manually — for advanced users.

device_picker.title: Select HA Device
device_picker.description: Select the Home Assistant device to build the virtual dS device from. You will then add its supported entities one by one.

device_entity_build.title: Add Entities to vdSD

device_entity_add.title: Select Entity to Add
device_entity_add.description: Pick one entity from the remaining supported entities on this HA device.

device_entity_user_input.title: Entity Options ({current} of {total})
device_entity_user_input.description: Entity: {entity_name} ({domain}). These settings tell digitalSTROM how to interpret this entity. The defaults work for most cases.

data.has_tilt: Device supports tilt / blade angle control

device_plan_summary.title: Device Plan Summary

device_model_features.title: Configurator Features — {vdsd_name} ({current} of {total})
device_model_features.description: These settings control which options appear in the digitalSTROM configurator for this device. Auto-derived selections are pre-filled based on your device configuration — adjust only if needed.

entity_picker.title: Select Entity
entity_picker.description: Choose the Home Assistant entity you want to expose to digitalSTROM. Only entities with a supported type are shown.

entity_user_input.title: Additional Configuration
entity_user_input.description: These settings tell digitalSTROM how to interpret this entity. The defaults work for most cases — check the descriptions if unsure.

data.has_tilt: Device supports tilt / blade angle control

entity_channel_mapping.title: Bind Output Channels to HA
entity_channel_mapping.description: For each output channel ({channels}): the 'read' entity is what digitalSTROM polls to get the current value; the 'write' action is called when digitalSTROM sends a command. The entity you selected is pre-filled as the read source.

device_info.title: Add Physical Device
device_info.description:
To create your device, consider splitting it into several logical vdSDs:
- Are parts physically separable? → use individual vdSDs.
- Does the (sub-)device contain several sensors? → keep as one vdSD.
- Does the (sub-)device contain different kinds of capabilities (Button, Binary Input, Sensors, Output)? → keep as one vdSD.
- Do functional units belong to different dS classes (colors)? → separate vdSDs.
- Can functional units end up in different zones? → separate vdSDs.
- Does the device contain several different outputs (not channels!)? → one vdSD per output required.
- Are there multiple buttons/binary inputs that shall be configurable via DSS configurator? → separate vdSDs (max. one (Multi)button and one binary input per vdSD).

data_description.vendorName: Manufacturer or vendor of the physical hardware device. Example: 'Occhio'.
data_description.displayId: Model or type name as printed on the device or used for identification. Example: 'Lunanova'.

data.identify_action: HA action to identify the device (optional)
data.firmwareUpdate_action: HA action to trigger firmware update (optional)

data_description.displayId: Name or label for this virtual device component as it will appear in the digitalSTROM configurator. Example: 'Lunanova PowerUnit'.
data_description.primaryGroup: Main dS color group for this component: Yellow=lights, Grey=blinds, Blue=climate.
data_description.modelVersion: Model variant string used to distinguish different hardware configurations.
data_description.identify_action: Home Assistant action to call when the dSS requests visual identification of this device.
data_description.firmwareUpdate_action: Home Assistant action to call when the dSS requests a firmware update for this device.

vdsd_overview.title: vdSD Overview — {vdsd_name}
vdsd_overview.description: Configure this virtual device component. Add buttons, binary inputs, sensors, and an output as needed. When done, click 'Next →' to choose configurator features.

data.name: Name of this button
data.channel: Output channel controlled by this button
data.supportsLocalKeyMode: Button can act as a local button
data.setsLocalPriority: Button sets local priority (ignores scene calls until cleared)
data.callsPresent: Button press sets presence state to 'present'
data.callbackType: How button presses are forwarded to dS

data_description.name: Human-readable label for this button, matching the physical hardware connector or panel label.
data_description.buttonType: Physical button type: single pushbutton, two-way rocker, 4-way navigation, etc.
data_description.group: dS color group this button controls, e.g. Yellow=lights, Grey=blinds.
data_description.function: Scope of the button's action: Room, Area 1–4, Apartment, etc.
data_description.mode: Button pairing and behavior mode. Use Standard for most buttons. AKM modes are for dry-contact inputs wired to a digitalSTROM contact module.
data_description.channel: Which output channel this button controls: 0=default channel.
data_description.supportsLocalKeyMode: If enabled, this button can operate locally on the device without a dSS connection.
data_description.setsLocalPriority: If enabled, pressing this button sets local priority on the output, causing it to ignore scene calls until priority is cleared.
data_description.callsPresent: If enabled, pressing this button switches the system presence state from absent to present.
data_description.callbackType: How button press events reach digitalSTROM: 'Click types' passes the raw click type from entity state; 'Auto-detect' recognises binary sensor, event, or button entities automatically.

data.name: Name of the binary input
data.group: dS group the binary input belongs to
data.hardwiredFunction: Is the detected function hardwired or configurable at runtime?
data.updateInterval: Time (in seconds) the hardware updates the value
data.inputType: Is the value polled by dSS or pushed at change?
data.inputUsage: Usage context of the binary input
data.valueType: Does the binary input deliver boolean or integer values?
data.callback_entity: Entity providing binary input state value

data_description.name: Human-readable label for this binary input, matching the physical hardware connector or panel label.
data_description.group: dS color group this binary input belongs to.
data_description.sensorFunction: What this binary input detects: e.g. presence, motion, smoke, window contact, window handle.
data_description.hardwiredFunction: If non-zero, the sensor function is fixed by hardware and cannot be changed at runtime. Value 0 means freely configurable.
data_description.updateInterval: How frequently the hardware tracks and reports the physical value, in seconds.
data_description.inputType: How the dSS receives the value: polled on request, or hardware pushes updates when the value changes.
data_description.inputUsage: Usage context of this input: room climate, outdoor climate, or climate setting.

data.name: Name of the sensor
data.resolution: Sensor resolution — smallest deviation that can be displayed (LSB)
data.updateInterval: Time (in seconds) the hardware tracks the sensor value
data.aliveSignInterval: Maximum time (in seconds) between updates before sensor is considered out of order
data.minPushInterval: Minimum time (in seconds) to wait between pushes of changed sensor values (default: 2 s)
data.changesOnlyInterval: Minimum time (in seconds) between pushes of unchanged values (0 = push all updates)

data_description.name: Human-readable label for this sensor, matching the physical hardware connector or panel label.
data_description.group: dS color group this sensor belongs to.
data_description.sensorType: Physical quantity this sensor measures: temperature, humidity, illuminance, CO₂, wind speed, active power, etc.
data_description.sensorUsage: Usage context: room, outdoor, user interaction, or device-level measurement.
data_description.min: Minimum physical value the sensor hardware can deliver.
data_description.max: Maximum physical value the sensor hardware can deliver.
data_description.resolution: Smallest value change the sensor hardware can detect — the size of one LSB of the hardware sensor.
data_description.updateInterval: Approximate time between hardware value updates in seconds.
data_description.aliveSignInterval: Maximum time in seconds between sensor updates before the sensor is considered out-of-order.
data_description.minPushInterval: Minimum time in seconds between consecutive push reports to dSS (throttle). Default is 2 seconds.
data_description.changesOnlyInterval: Minimum time in seconds between pushes of the same (unchanged) value. Default 0 means every update triggers a push.

data.name: Name for this output
data.defaultGroup: Default color group of the output

data_description.name: Human-readable label for this output, matching the physical hardware connector or panel label.
data_description.groups: Set of dS color groups this output can operate in, e.g. Yellow=lights, Grey=blinds. An output may belong to multiple groups.
data_description.defaultGroup: Primary dS color group for this output, used when no explicit group is specified.
data_description.function: Output capability: on/off only, dimmer, positional (blinds/valves), dimmer with color temperature, full color (RGB+CT), or bipolar (heating/cooling valve).
data_description.outputUsage: Usage context of this output: room, outdoors, or user display.
data_description.variableRamp: If enabled, this output supports configurable transition speeds (dim time settings become meaningful).
data_description.mode: Operating mode: disabled, binary (on/off), or gradual (continuous range).

data.onThreshold: Minimum output level that switches the output on (default: 50%)
data.minBrightness: Minimum output level the hardware supports (e.g. ignition threshold for bulbs)
data.maxPower: Maximum power the output can deliver (W)
data.activeCoolingMode: Output provides active cooling capabilities (e.g. air-conditioning, fan coil unit)

data_description.onThreshold: The brightness level (0–100%) at which the output switches ON. Defaults to 50%.
data_description.minBrightness: Minimum brightness level (0–100%) the hardware supports.
data_description.maxPower: Maximum power output in watts. Leave empty if unknown.
data_description.activeCoolingMode: If enabled, this output can actively cool (e.g. air conditioning unit).

data.name: Name of this channel
data.resolution: Channel resolution — smallest deviation that can be displayed (LSB)

data_description.channelType: Type of this control channel as defined by digitalSTROM: brightness, hue, saturation, color temperature, position, angle, etc.
data_description.name: Human-readable label for this output channel.
data_description.min: Minimum physical value this channel can accept or deliver.
data_description.max: Maximum physical value this channel can accept or deliver.
data_description.resolution: Smallest step this channel can resolve — the finest granularity of control available.

channel_mapping.title: Bind Output Channels to HA
channel_mapping.description: Select the entities that reflect the channel values of the output.

name_confirm.title: Confirm Device Names
name_confirm.description: Review and confirm the names for this virtual device. These names appear in Home Assistant and in the digitalSTROM configurator.

vdsd_name.title: Name this Device
vdsd_name.description: This virtual device has multiple elements. Enter a name that will appear both in Home Assistant and in the digitalSTROM configurator.

data_description.name: Name shown in the Home Assistant device page and in the digitalSTROM configurator.

model_features.title: Configurator Features
model_features.description: These settings control which options appear in the digitalSTROM configurator for this device. Auto-derived selections are pre-filled based on your device configuration — adjust only if needed.

entity_completion.title: Complete Device — {device_name}
entity_completion.description:
Configured components: {vdsds}

Choose how to proceed.

menu_options.entity_completion_add: Add another component from an HA entity

device_summary.title: Device Summary — {device_name}
device_summary.description:
Virtual device components: {vdsds}

Choose how to proceed.

error.entity_not_supported: This entity type / device class combination is not supported.

auto.dontcare: Scene editor: adds 'keep current value' checkbox — device ignores scene changes
auto.blink: Scene editor: adds blink effect option per scene
auto.transt: Scene editor: adds transition speed selector (standard / slow) per scene
auto.outvalue8: Scene editor: adds output brightness slider (0–100%) per scene
auto.outputchannels: Scene editor: adds individual sliders for each output channel (colour, temperature, etc.)
auto.shadeposition: Scene editor: adds position slider and up/down buttons for shades
auto.shadebladeang: Scene editor: adds blade angle slider for jalousie slat control
auto.dimtimeconfig: Device config: adds ramp time settings (how fast the output dims up and down)
auto.outconfigswitch: Device config: adds threshold setting at which the output switches on/off
auto.impulseconfig: Device config: adds impulse output mode tab
auto.pwmvalue: Device config: shows output value as PWM duty cycle
auto.ventconfig: Device config: adds ventilation fan speed steps and flap angle settings
auto.shadeprops: Device config: adds shade travel timing (time to fully open / close)
auto.motiontimefins: Device config: adds blade motion timing in shade properties
auto.locationconfig: Device config: adds orientation/direction dropdown (e.g. south facade)
auto.operationlock: Device config: allows weather alarms to override the operation lock on this device
auto.windprotectionconfigblind: Device config: adds wind protection class for jalousie / blind
auto.windprotectionconfigawning: Device config: adds wind protection class for awning / roller blind
auto.heatingprops: Device config: adds valve type, PWM period and heating zone settings
auto.heatinggroup: Device config: adds heating group assignment dropdown
auto.valvetype: Device config: adds attached valve or terminal device type selector
auto.extendedvalvetypes: Device config: adds extended valve type options beyond the standard set
auto.fcu: Device config: adds fan coil unit settings (fan speed, heating/cooling/ventilation modes)
auto.temperatureoffset: Device config: adds temperature correction offset field
auto.consumption: Device config: adds energy consumption monitoring and events menu
auto.akmsensor: Device config: adds selector for what the dry-contact input detects (presence, motion, window open, etc.)
auto.pushbutton: Device config: adds button type assignment selector (single, rocker, 4-way, etc.)
auto.pushbadvanced: Device config: adds per-scene click-type configuration and local priority toggle
auto.pushbdisabled: Device config: adds dialog to deactivate unused button elements
auto.jokerconfig: Device config: adds color group assignment for flexible/joker-class devices
auto.pushbarea: Button config: adds 'area' as a selectable button function type
auto.pushbdevice: Button config: adds 'device' as a selectable button function type
auto.pushbsensor: Button config: adds 'sensor-style' as a selectable button function type
auto.highlevel: Button config: adds 'app / scene trigger' as a selectable button function type
auto.identification: Adds an 'Identify' entry — triggers a visual signal on the physical device from the configurator

optional.blinkconfig: Device config: adds blink pattern and behaviour settings (not tested)
optional.customtransitiontime: Scene editor: adds a custom transition time field per scene (not tested)
optional.consumptiontimer: Device config: adds consumption timer and run-time tracking panel (not tested)
optional.outmodegeneric: Device config: adds output mode selector with generic values 0–6 (not tested)
optional.outmodeauto: Device config: adds 'Auto' option to the output mode selector (not tested)
optional.jokertempcontrol: Enables temperature-controlled output behaviour for flexible/joker-class devices (not tested)
optional.umvrelay: Device config: adds relay function selector for universal module hardware (not tested)
optional.ftwtempcontrolventilationselect: Device config: adds combined temperature and ventilation mode selector (not tested)
optional.setumr200config: Device config: adds hardware configuration for UMR200 module (not tested)
optional.apartmentapplication: Enables apartment-level application integration for this device (not tested)
optional.customactivityconfig: Adds custom activity and app configuration options (not tested)

creation_mode.from_entity: Create device based on HA entities (recommended)
creation_mode.from_ha_device: Create device based on HA device

state_files_action.keep: Use existing state (restore saved device configuration)

callback_type.clickTypes: Click types (entity state = click type number)
callback_type.actionIds: Scene / action IDs (entity state = scene number)
